fill: U bent ___ een zorgverzekering te hebben.
fill_en: You are ___________ to have health insurance.

sentence.nl: U bent verplicht dit formulier in te vullen.
sentence.en: You are required to fill in this form.

info.translation: obligatory, required — you have no choice
info.tip: 🔑 When you see verplicht something is mandatory. The opposite is vrijwillig (voluntary).

fill: U ___ roken in dit gebouw.
fill_en: You ___________ smoke in this building.

sentence.nl: U mag niet parkeren voor de ingang.
sentence.en: You must not park in front of the entrance.

info.translation: is not allowed, must not — a prohibition

parts.meaning: not — turns permission into a ban

info.tip: 🔑 Mag niet = forbidden. Compare: hoeft niet = doesn't need to (but could). A huge difference!

fill: U ___ te betalen – dit is gratis.
fill_en: You ___________ to pay – this is free.

sentence.nl: U hoeft niet te bellen – u kunt ook mailen.
sentence.en: You don't need to call – you can also email.

info.translation: does not need to — no obligation, but still permitted
info.tip: 🔑 Hoeft niet = optional. You're free to skip it. Don't confuse with mag niet (forbidden).

fill: De huurder ___ de schade te melden.
fill_en: The tenant ___________ report the damage.

sentence.nl: U dient te verschijnen op het opgegeven adres.
sentence.en: You are required to appear at the given address.

info.translation: must, is required to — formal version of 'moet'

parts.meaning: connects to the next verb (like 'to' in English)

info.tip: 🔑 Dient te = formal moet. Very common in official letters. Always followed by an infinitive verb.

fill: U ___ twee weken vakantie per jaar.
fill_en: You ___________ two weeks of holiday per year.

sentence.nl: U heeft recht op een vergoeding.
sentence.en: You are entitled to a reimbursement.

info.translation: to be entitled to, to have a right to

parts.meaning: on / to — links 'right' to what follows

info.tip: 🔑 Key phrase in benefit texts. If you see recht op, the text is telling you what you can claim.

fill: Niet iedereen kan ___ voor deze toeslag.
fill_en: Not everyone can ___________ for this benefit.

sentence.nl: Komt u in aanmerking voor huurtoeslag?
sentence.en: Do you qualify for rent allowance?

info.translation: to qualify, to be eligible — literally 'to come into consideration'
info.tip: 🔑 Very common in subsidy texts. Answers: who is eligible? Often followed by voor + the benefit.

en: at the latest / no later than
fill: Stuur het formulier ___ vrijdag op.
fill_en: Send the form no later than ___________ Friday.

sentence.nl: U moet uiterlijk 1 juni reageren.
sentence.en: You must respond no later than 1 June.

info.translation: at the latest, no later than — gives a hard deadline
info.tip: 🔑 Uiterlijk = the deadline you cannot miss. Do this BY this date, not after.

fill: ___ 1 januari geldt de nieuwe regel.
fill_en: ___________ 1 January the new rule applies.

sentence.nl: Vanaf maandag zijn wij gesloten.
sentence.en: From Monday we will be closed.

info.translation: from, starting from — marks the start of a period

parts.meaning: off / away — adds the idea of a starting point

info.tip: 🔑 Vanaf tells you WHEN something starts. Pair with tot en met to get the full time range.

fill: De aanbieding geldt ___ zaterdag.
fill_en: The offer is valid ___________ Saturday.

sentence.nl: De cursus loopt tot en met vrijdag.
sentence.en: The course runs up to and including Friday.

info.translation: up to and including — the end date IS included

parts.meaning: and with — the end date is included

info.tip: 🔑 Critical: tot zaterdag = until Friday night (Saturday excluded). Tot en met zaterdag = Saturday is the last day and IS included.

fill: U ontvangt een reactie ___ twee weken.
fill_en: You will receive a response ___________ two weeks.

sentence.nl: Betaal de rekening binnen dertig dagen.
sentence.en: Pay the invoice within thirty days.

info.translation: within — the action must happen inside this time window
info.tip: 🔑 Binnen + time = you have that long to act. Common in letters: binnen zes weken = you have six weeks.

fill: ___ van uw brief sturen wij een bevestiging.
fill_en: ___________ your letter we will send a confirmation.

sentence.nl: Na ontvangst verwerken wij uw aanvraag.
sentence.en: Upon receipt we will process your application.

info.translation: upon receipt — as soon as we receive it
info.tip: 🔑 Na ontvangst signals when something happens — right after they get your document.

fill: Neem ___ contact met ons op.
fill_en: Please contact us ___________.

sentence.nl: Wij verzoeken u zo spoedig mogelijk te reageren.
sentence.en: We ask you to respond as soon as possible.

info.translation: as soon as possible — urgency without a fixed deadline
info.tip: 🔑 Formal ASAP. Less strict than uiterlijk (fixed date), but still urgent.

fill: U krijgt korting, ___ u lid bent.
fill_en: You get a discount, ___________ you are a member.

sentence.nl: U mag gebruik maken van de dienst, mits u zich aanmeldt.
sentence.en: You may use the service, provided that you register.

info.translation: provided that, as long as — a condition that must be met

parts.meaning: from old Dutch 'met dien (dat)' = with the condition that

info.tip: 🔑 Mits introduces a requirement. If not met, the main statement doesn't apply. Positive condition (compare: tenzij = negative exception).

fill: De winkel is open, ___ het een feestdag is.
fill_en: The shop is open, ___________ it is a public holiday.

sentence.nl: U hoeft niet te betalen, tenzij u te laat bent.
sentence.en: You don't need to pay, unless you are late.

info.translation: unless — introduces the ONE exception that changes everything
info.tip: 🔑 The rule holds in ALL cases EXCEPT the one mentioned after tenzij. Opposite of mits.

fill: U krijgt de subsidie ___ u aan de eisen voldoet.
fill_en: You receive the subsidy ___________ you meet the requirements.

sentence.nl: U kunt meedoen, op voorwaarde dat u ingeschreven bent.
sentence.en: You can participate, on the condition that you are registered.

info.translation: on the condition that — formal, stronger version of 'mits'
info.tip: 🔑 Longer and more formal than mits. Common in contracts. Everything before it is only true if what follows is met.

fill: ___ u vragen heeft, kunt u bellen.
fill_en: ___________ you have questions, you can call.

sentence.nl: Indien u niet aanwezig kunt zijn, laat het ons weten.
sentence.en: If you are unable to attend, please let us know.

info.translation: if — formal written version of 'als'
info.tip: 🔑 Indien = formal als. Spoken Dutch uses 'als'. In official letters and exam texts you always see 'indien'.

fill: De hoogte van de toeslag is ___ uw inkomen.
fill_en: The amount of the benefit is ___________ your income.

sentence.nl: Het bedrag is afhankelijk van uw situatie.
sentence.en: The amount depends on your situation.

info.translation: depending on — the outcome varies based on a factor
info.tip: 🔑 When you see afhankelijk van, look for the variable — usually income, household size, or situation.

fill: De aanvraag was goedgekeurd. ___ moest er nog een document worden ingeleverd.
fill_en: The application was approved. ___________ another document still had to be submitted.

sentence.nl: De cursus is gratis. Echter, u moet zich wel aanmelden.
sentence.en: The course is free. However, you do need to register.

info.translation: however — signals a contrast or complication
info.tip: 🔑 Echter is a warning: the sentence before seemed fine, but now there's a 'but'. Pay close attention to what follows.

fill: De woning is groot. ___ is de huur laag.
fill_en: The house is large. ___________ the rent is low.

sentence.nl: U krijgt een vergoeding. Bovendien is de cursus gratis.
sentence.en: You get a reimbursement. Moreover, the course is free.

info.translation: moreover, furthermore — adds something extra on top
info.tip: 🔑 Bovendien adds a bonus point to reinforce what was just said. Both things point in the same direction.

fill: Er waren problemen. ___ ging de vergadering door.
fill_en: There were problems. ___________ the meeting continued.

sentence.nl: Het was ingewikkeld. Desondanks lukte het.
sentence.en: It was complicated. Nevertheless, it worked out.

info.translation: nevertheless, despite this — the obstacle didn't stop the outcome
info.tip: 🔑 Something happened even though there was a reason it might not have. The obstacle existed but was overcome.

en: on the other hand / by contrast
fill: De ene cursus is duur. De andere ___ is gratis.
fill_en: One course is expensive. The other ___________ is free.

sentence.nl: Werken geeft inkomen. Studeren daarentegen kost geld.
sentence.en: Working gives income. Studying, on the other hand, costs money.

info.translation: on the other hand, by contrast — introduces the opposite side
info.tip: 🔑 The second thing is the opposite of the first. Used to contrast two options, people, or situations.

fill: U hoeft niet te komen – u bent ___ al ingeschreven.
fill_en: You don't need to come – you are ___________ already registered.

sentence.nl: U heeft geen pas nodig, u bent immers lid.
sentence.en: You don't need a pass – you are, after all, a member.

info.translation: after all, as you know — refers to a fact assumed to be already known
info.tip: 🔑 Immers gives the reason why something is true — treating it as obvious or shared knowledge.

fill: U mag ___ drie boeken tegelijk lenen.
fill_en: You may borrow ___________ three books at a time.

sentence.nl: U kunt maximaal twee weken afwezig zijn.
sentence.en: You can be absent for a maximum of two weeks.

info.translation: at most, maximum — you cannot exceed this number
info.tip: 🔑 Sets the upper limit. You can do less, but never more.

fill: U moet ___ zes maanden in Nederland wonen.
fill_en: You must live in the Netherlands for ___________ six months.

sentence.nl: U moet minimaal achtien jaar oud zijn.
sentence.en: You must be at least eighteen years old.

info.translation: at least, minimum — the lowest acceptable amount
info.tip: 🔑 Sets the lower limit — you must reach at least this. Opposite: maximaal.

fill: De boete bedraagt ___ €500.
fill_en: The fine amounts to ___________ €500.

sentence.nl: U mag ten hoogste één keer per jaar aanvragen.
sentence.en: You may apply at most once per year.

info.translation: at most — formal written version of 'maximaal'

parts.meaning: highest → at the highest = at most

info.tip: 🔑 Ten hoogste = formal maximaal. Often used in legal texts to cap a fine or frequency.

fill: U dient ___ twee weken van tevoren te annuleren.
fill_en: You must cancel ___________ two weeks in advance.

sentence.nl: U moet ten minste drie jaar ingeschreven zijn.
sentence.en: You must be registered for at least three years.

info.translation: at least — formal written version of 'minimaal'

parts.meaning: least → at the least = at least

info.tip: 🔑 Ten minste = formal minimaal. Sets the floor — the minimum requirement.

fill: U moet ___ een geldig identiteitsbewijs.
fill_en: You must ___________ a valid identity document.

sentence.nl: U dient te beschikken over een werkvergunning.
sentence.en: You must possess a work permit.

info.translation: to have at one's disposal, to possess — formal 'hebben'

parts.meaning: intensifier — makes the verb act on an object
over / about — links to what you possess

info.tip: 🔑 Beschikken over = formal 'to have'. You need to ALREADY have it — not apply for it.

fill: U kunt een toeslag ___ bij de Belastingdienst.
fill_en: You can ___________ a benefit at the Tax Authority.

sentence.nl: U kunt een uitkering aanvragen bij het UWV.
sentence.en: You can apply for a benefit at the UWV.

info.translation: to apply for — to formally request something
info.tip: 🔑 Separable verb — the particle splits off: Ik vraag een uitkering aan. Means to formally request from an authority.

fill: U kunt bezwaar ___ binnen zes weken.
fill_en: You can ___________ an objection within six weeks.

sentence.nl: U kunt een klacht indienen bij de gemeente.
sentence.en: You can submit a complaint to the municipality.

info.translation: to submit, to file — handing something in formally
info.tip: 🔑 Separable: Ik dien een klacht in. More official than inleveren. Used for complaints, appeals, applications.

fill: U moet ___ de gestelde eisen.
fill_en: You must ___________ the stated requirements.

sentence.nl: U moet voldoen aan de voorwaarden.
sentence.en: You must comply with the conditions.

info.translation: to meet, to satisfy, to comply with — conditions or requirements

parts.meaning: to / at — links to the requirement

info.tip: 🔑 Common in eligibility criteria. If you don't voldoen aan the requirements, you don't qualify.

fill: U kunt ___ de gratis service.
fill_en: You can ___________ the free service.

sentence.nl: U kunt gebruik maken van de regeling.
sentence.en: You can make use of the scheme.

info.translation: to make use of, to take advantage of

parts.meaning: of / from — links to what is being used

info.tip: 🔑 Signals a benefit or service available to you. Often means: here is something you are allowed to use.

fill: U ontvangt binnenkort een ___ van de gemeente.
fill_en: You will soon receive a ___________ from the municipality.

sentence.nl: In de beschikking staat uw recht op toeslag.
sentence.en: The official decision states your entitlement to benefit.

info.translation: official decision — a formal ruling by an authority about your situation
info.tip: 🔑 A beschikking is the document where an authority announces their decision. You have 6 weeks to file bezwaar if you disagree.

fill: Bent u het niet eens? Dan kunt u ___ tegen dit besluit.
fill_en: Do you disagree? Then you can ___________ against this decision.

sentence.nl: U kunt bezwaar maken binnen zes weken.
sentence.en: You can appeal within six weeks.

info.translation: to object, to appeal — formally disagreeing with an official decision

parts.meaning: to make → 'to make something heavy' = to raise an objection

info.tip: 🔑 In the Netherlands you usually have 6 weeks from the date of the beschikking to file bezwaar.

fill: De ___ voor aanvragen is 1 april.
fill_en: The ___________ for applications is 1 April.

sentence.nl: Let op de termijn van zes weken.
sentence.en: Pay attention to the six-week deadline.

info.translation: deadline, time limit, term — the window you must act within
info.tip: 🔑 De termijn verstrijkt = the deadline passes. Very common in letters about appeals, payments, permits.

fill: Wij schrijven u ___ uw aanvraag.
fill_en: We are writing to you ___________ your application.

sentence.nl: Wij sturen u informatie betreffende uw contract.
sentence.en: We are sending you information regarding your contract.

info.translation: regarding, concerning — tells you what the document is about

parts.meaning: to concern, to hit, to meet

info.tip: 🔑 Often in the subject line of formal letters. It immediately tells you the topic of the document.

fill: ___ ontvangt u de bevestiging van uw afspraak.
fill_en: ___________ you receive the confirmation of your appointment.

sentence.nl: Hierbij sturen wij u het formulier.
sentence.en: Herewith we send you the form.

info.translation: herewith, enclosed — 'with this letter I am sending you...'
info.tip: 🔑 Hierbij opens many formal letters. Whatever follows is the main action or information being sent.

fill: De vergoeding is vastgesteld ___ de wet.
fill_en: The reimbursement has been determined ___________ the law.

sentence.nl: Wij handelen conform de regels.
sentence.en: We act in accordance with the rules.

info.translation: in accordance with, compliant with — following a rule or law

parts.meaning: in the form of / in line with

info.tip: 🔑 Conform = formal volgens. Signals that procedure is being followed exactly as required.

fill: De winkel is elke dag open, ___ op zondag.
fill_en: The shop is open every day, ___________ on Sunday.

sentence.nl: Iedereen is welkom, behalve kinderen onder twaalf.
sentence.en: Everyone is welcome, except children under twelve.

info.translation: except, apart from — introduces the one thing excluded
info.tip: 🔑 The general rule applies to everything EXCEPT what follows. The exception is often the key detail being tested.

fill: Dit punt is ___ op uw situatie.
fill_en: This point is ___________ to your situation.

sentence.nl: Deze regel is niet van toepassing op studenten.
sentence.en: This rule is not applicable to students.

info.translation: not applicable — this rule/condition does not apply to your case
info.tip: 🔑 Abbreviated as n.v.t. on forms. This section or rule does not concern your case — skip it.

fill: Alle medewerkers komen, ___ de directeur.
fill_en: All employees are coming, ___________ the director.

sentence.nl: Alle documenten zijn digitaal, uitgezonderd het paspoort.
sentence.en: All documents are digital, with the exception of the passport.

info.translation: with the exception of — more formal than 'behalve'
info.tip: 🔑 Uitgezonderd = more formal behalve. Everything on a list EXCEPT what is named after this word.

fill: ___ uw inkomen heeft u recht op deze zorg.
fill_en: ___________ your income you are entitled to this care.

sentence.nl: U heeft recht op hulp, ongeacht uw nationaliteit.
sentence.en: You are entitled to help, regardless of your nationality.

info.translation: regardless of — a factor that is explicitly ignored
info.tip: 🔑 Ongeacht = applies to EVERYONE, no exceptions based on what follows. Opposite of afhankelijk van.
